text: Four trocars are placed in the abdominal wall. The mass is visible with its attachment to the dome of the bladder. The mesentery is dissected using a hook cautery. A mesenteric window is opened, isolating the inferior mesenteric artery and vein. The artery is ligated with two hemoclips and cut, followed by the vein, which is also ligated with hemoclips and cut using an energy device. Dissection continues laterally with an incision along the white line of Toldt, mobilizing the colon. The dome of the bladder, invaded by cancer, is included in the specimen to ensure safe surgical margins.

The surgeon transects the rectum using surgical staplers. The superior wall of the bladder is repaired with a single layer continuous suture. A colorectal anastomosis is performed using a circular stapler, followed by a negative air leak test indicating no leaks.
